what_it_does: This file specifies a single router/wire that joins an existing direction-capture event to an existing generative brain and an existing addressed registry write, so a user direction at an address can generate or mutate content at that address and trigger a re-render.
observations: It is a SPEC ONLY document; nothing is built from it and it is gated on Tim's build-go.
It names 'The One Wire' as the MAKE verb under composition's V VERB CONTRACT.
MAKE is aim-type-gated: enabled only on writable addresses (vi-vision assets or real data nodes), disabled on synthetic ui:// instrument sectors.
It lists already-built pieces: direction capture, retarget-by-address, run_turn brain, territory_prose context composer, territory_write registry, and resolve_address.
The only new connection is a router step that routes generative direction items (do_this / direction) through run_turn before writing the brain output to the registry.
Resolved sub-decisions include: generate also leaves a provenance mark, async shimmer-to-solid feedback, start with do_this only, and write is lead-governed not brain-unilateral.
It maps the same wire to multiple per-surface write-targets: gallery unit, DNA-system, mockup-content, and composition factory.
It defines verify-by-use proof steps and assigns ownership at the seam to wildcard, fork, and DNA/composition.
standouts: The 'ONE new thing' is described as a ~1-function router/join, not new infrastructure.
MAKE is explicitly gated on writable aim type per an external verb contract.
Retargetability comes from using an address as the target rather than a hardcoded surface.
conventions: Uses a structured SPEC with authors, status, evidence, and decision tables.
Relies on an addressed-registry / address-scheme abstraction for routing and context.
Reconciles with an external verb contract and folds in its constraints.
Assigns neutral ownership at subsystem seams.
Uses tables to enumerate built pieces and per-surface write targets.
concerns: Some pieces are marked 'built' based on fork reports that the file itself notes need fork confirmation.
The router is described as simple but the file does not define error handling, idempotency, or payload-shape validation for brain output.
The build-go is centralized on a single person ('Tim steers whether/when it's built').
The writable-aim predicate depends on resolve_address semantics that are not defined in this file.
notes: A concise spec for the final join that turns existing direction-capture events into generative writes at addressed surfaces, gated on writable aim types and a named build-go.
questions: What exactly makes a direction/do_this item 'generative' versus a plain mark — is it only the type or also the text content?
How does the router validate or handle brain output that does not fit the target address's expected payload shape?
What is the precise, testable definition of a 'writable' address and who exposes that predicate?
How are failures, retries, or cancellation of the async shimmer-to-solid cycle surfaced to the user?
What provenance link is stored between the generated write and the original direction mark?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify the one join between direction capture and generative write, and the file does that while also providing context tables, verification steps, and ownership assignments.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define the smallest possible integration contract that lets a user's direction trigger generative mutation at any addressed surface, while aligning with an existing verb contract and preventing unilateral brain writes.
novelty: It treats a generative self-build capability as a single 'wire' or router join across already-built subsystems, and explicitly gates the MAKE verb on writable aim types rather than treating every direction as generative.
contribution: Provides a concrete, minimal spec for the direction-to-generation router, including the aim-type gate, per-surface address mapping, verification criteria, and ownership seams, which a fused system could keep as the integration contract.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a SPEC that defines 'The One Wire,' a single router join routing existing direction-capture events through an existing generative brain to an existing addressed registry write, implementing the MAKE verb under an external verb contract and gated to writable aim types.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to specify the minimal, retargetable integration that lets user directions generate or mutate assets at addressed surfaces, pending Tim's approval to build.
